statute: SDCL § 10-46A (Contractors Excise Tax License); local municipal ordinances govern licensing
license_label: South Dakota Contractor's Excise Tax License Number
license_notes: SD licensing is mostly local. ALL contractors must obtain a Contractor's Excise Tax License from SD Department of Revenue. Electrical and plumbing require state licenses through Dept. of Labor and Regulation. Missing excise tax license = Class 1 misdemeanor.

mandatory_clauses.label: SD excise tax license disclosure
Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under state law, contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: Missing excise tax license = Class 1 misdemeanor ($1,000 fine/1 year jail). Continuing after notification = felony ($4,000 fine/2 years prison). Local penalties vary.
notes: South Dakota requires ALL contractors to have a Contractor's Excise Tax License from the Department of Revenue. This is separate from (and in addition to) any local licensing. Electrical and plumbing require state licenses. Local licensing varies by city (Sioux Falls, Rapid City have their own requirements). Continuing to operate after notification of violation = felony.
